groups.name: Process Engineer
groups.resp: Owns individual process modules (lithography, etch, CVD, CMP, implant, RTA). Executes SPC, DOE, recipe development and optimization, defect excursion response. Responsible for module-level yield and electrical targets.

groups.name: Process Integration Engineer
groups.resp: Coordinates across all process modules to ensure full-flow integration yield. Owns integration flow document, split-lot design, process window, and APC/SPC strategy across the full device flow. Interfaces with customers on product requirements.

groups.name: PDK & Design Technology Platform Engineer
groups.resp: Develops and maintains the Process Design Kit (SPICE models, DRC/LVS/PEX, standard cells, Liberty .lib). Calibrates electrical models to silicon data. Supports customer tape-out and co-develops with EDA vendors.

groups.name: Equipment Engineer
groups.resp: Owns tool uptime, preventive maintenance, spare parts, and performance for all process and metrology tools. Leads FAT and SAT procedures. Manages tool-to-tool matching, FDC rule deployment, and equipment qualification.

groups.name: Metrology Engineer
groups.resp: Owns all inline and offline measurement programs: CD-SEM, overlay, film thickness, defect inspection. Conducts MSA/GR&R studies. Provides calibrated data for SPC, model calibration, and yield correlation. Develops virtual metrology models.

groups.name: Quality & Reliability Engineer
groups.resp: Manages QMS (ISO 9001, IATF 16949). Executes reliability qualification tests (HTOL, HAST, TC per JEDEC). Performs failure analysis. Calculates FIT rates. Manages DPPM tracking, customer PQE interface, FMEA, and corrective actions.

groups.name: IT & Automation Engineer
groups.resp: Designs, deploys, and operates MES, FDC, APC/R2R, SECS/GEM, AMHS dispatch, YMS, and data infrastructure. Implements cybersecurity per IEC 62443. Manages DMS (Document Management System) and data governance.

groups.name: Facility & Utility Engineer
groups.resp: Plans, constructs, operates, and maintains all fab utility systems: UPW, bulk/specialty gas, chemical distribution, HVAC, power/UPS, abatement, EHS. Certifies cleanroom to ISO 14644-1. Manages capacity expansion.

phases.long: Phase 0 — Preparation: Technology Review, IP Scope, Contract & Infrastructure Setup
phases.desc: Evaluate technology transfer proposals, conduct IP due diligence, define the IP portfolio scope, negotiate and finalize the Technology Transfer Agreement (TTA), and prepare all enabling infrastructure: DMS (Document Management System), IT architecture, team structure, and procurement framework. The IP definitions and contract terms established here govern the entire project lifetime and all downstream licensing, royalty, and legal obligations.
phases.entry: Business decision to establish new fab confirmed; Technology Transfer Partner (TTP) shortlisted; budget approved; project steering committee formed.
phases.exit: TTA signed and registered; IP scope (Schedule A) finalized and agreed; DMS operational and document taxonomy defined; team fully recruited; construction and equipment procurement plans approved; Vietnamese MOST technology transfer registration dossier submitted.

phases.long: Phase 1 Stream A — Technology Transfer: Deliverable Audit, Co-Development & Training at TTP
phases.desc: [PARALLEL STREAM — runs simultaneously with P1b and P1c] Receive all technology transfer deliverables from TTP. Audit each deliverable for completeness and accuracy against the agreed IP scope (Schedule A). Co-develop additional IPs or test vehicles with TTP where gaps are identified. Conduct structured on-site training at TTP's fab for process, integration, PDK, metrology, and quality engineers. Establish IP completeness baseline before fab readiness for first wafer.
phases.entry: TTA signed; TTP's internal transfer team mobilized; Green Fab engineers recruited and available for training; DMS ready to receive classified documents.
phases.exit: All T1 (Must Transfer) IP deliverables received and audited as complete; all T2 (Should Transfer) IPs received or gap-closure plan agreed; training sessions at TTP completed for all engineering groups; IP completeness audit report signed off by both parties.

phases.long: Phase 1 Stream B — Equipment & Material: PO, Co-development, FAT, Hook-Up & SAT
phases.desc: [PARALLEL STREAM — runs simultaneously with P1a and P1c] Issue Purchase Orders to equipment and material vendors. Co-develop equipment process recipes and specifications with vendors to match TTP's transferred process requirements. Conduct training at vendor sites. Execute Factory Acceptance Tests (FAT) at each vendor. Manage equipment delivery and cleanroom move-in. Hook up all utilities and establish SECS/GEM automation integration. Execute Site Acceptance Tests (SAT) and transfer tool ownership to Green Fab.
phases.entry: Equipment vendor list finalized; PO budget approved; key equipment specifications defined from TTP technology transfer; cleanroom construction at sufficient completion stage for delivery.
phases.exit: All critical-path tools hooked up and SAT-accepted; SECS/GEM connections to MES verified; tool-to-tool matching within specification; FDC baseline deployed; all utility connections at tool points verified; tool qualification (P2 entry) ready to begin.

phases.long: Phase 1 Stream C — Fab Construction: Feasibility, Design, Construction & Facility Readiness Test
phases.desc: [PARALLEL STREAM — runs simultaneously with P1a and P1b] Conduct site feasibility study and select optimal fab location. Develop cleanroom architectural design consistent with process requirements (airflow class, contamination zones, vibration specs). Execute construction of cleanroom shell, sub-fab, and utility systems. Commission all utility systems (UPW, gas, chemical distribution, HVAC, power, abatement). Conduct Facility Readiness Test (FRT) to certify ISO cleanroom class and utility specs before tool installation begins.
phases.entry: Site selected; environmental permits obtained; architect and construction contractor engaged; fab design specifications defined from process and equipment requirements.
phases.exit: Cleanroom certified to ISO 14644-1 target class (particle count, airflow); UPW, gas, chemical, power, HVAC, and abatement systems commissioned to specification; Facility Readiness Test (FRT) signed off; cleanroom ready to receive process tools.

phases.short: P2: Test Vehicle, PDK v0.9 & Yield 60%
phases.long: Phase 2 — Test Vehicle, PDK v0.9 Development & Process Qualification (Yield Target ≥ 60%)
phases.desc: Run test vehicle wafers (including designed test patterns and test chip structures) through the complete process flow to validate and qualify all process modules on Green Fab's specific tools. Iteratively optimize process recipes based on electrical and yield data. Build PDK v0.9 based on Green Fab's actual equipment and calibrated process integration flow. Release PDK v0.9 to early customer design teams. Conduct yield learning, defect playbook development, and FMEA on test vehicle data. Target: achieve functional yield ≥ 60% after full flow on test vehicle wafers. [Industry benchmark: TSMC achieved ~60% on first 2nm lots; commercially viable yield begins at ~70-80% for mature nodes — LinkedIn/Kamaly 2025]
phases.entry: All critical-path tools SAT-accepted (P1b complete); cleanroom certified (P1c complete); all T1 IP deliverables received (P1a complete); process recipes loaded on Green Fab tools; basic PDK framework available.
phases.exit: Functional yield ≥ 60% on test vehicle wafer (full flow, multiple lots); PDK v0.9 released to at least one customer design team; process corners (SS/TT/FF) defined and characterized; SPICE models calibrated to Green Fab silicon; defect playbook started (top-10 defect modes classified); FMEA completed for all critical process modules; SPC, FDC, and APC systems operational.

phases.short: P3: Qualified Product, PDK v1.0 & HVM (Yield 80%)
phases.long: Phase 3 — Qualified Product, PDK v1.0, Complete Documentation & High-Volume Manufacturing (Yield Target ≥ 80%)
phases.desc: Optimize process and integration flow for customer product designs to achieve production yield ≥ 80%. Complete all reliability qualification testing (HTOL/HAST/TC per JEDEC). Finalize and release PDK v1.0 with complete production-grade models, rule decks, and IP library. Complete all process documentation and know-how transfer. Obtain customer PQE (Product Quality Engineer) acceptance and JEDEC qualification sign-off. Ramp to high-volume manufacturing (HVM). [Industry benchmark: 'commercially viable yields above 70-80% for mature nodes' — Kamaly 2025; GF 14nm HVM used systematic defect methodology to close yield gap — ASMC 2022]
phases.entry: Test vehicle yield ≥ 60% (P2 exit); PDK v0.9 released and customer test chip taped out; reliability qualification lots started; process recipes locked (no experiments).
phases.exit: Customer product yield ≥ 80% (wafer sort, multiple consecutive lots); PDK v1.0 released (production-grade); HTOL/HAST/TC reliability qualification passed (JEDEC/AEC-Q100); FIT rate calculated and reported; customer PQE sign-off obtained; Certificate of Conformance (CoC) issued; HVM production ramp established at committed wafer start rate.
